parsed.answer: Diamonds are thermodynamically metastable at Earth's surface conditions and will theoretically convert to graphite over extremely long timescales, but this process is so slow it is effectively negligible on any human or geological timescale. Practically speaking, diamonds last billions to trillions of years — essentially forever for all practical purposes. The conversion rate to graphite at room temperature is immeasurably slow.
